depression.description: I can't shake off the feeling that I failed my aunt by not being able to fulfill her last request. It haunts me daily, and I feel like I'm a bad person because of it.
I often find myself putting everyone else's needs before my own, and it leaves me feeling drained and unfulfilled. I know I should take care of myself, but I feel guilty even thinking about it.
I feel this overwhelming pressure to be there for everyone, and when I can't, I feel worthless. It's like my whole identity is tied to how much I can help others.
Sometimes I feel like I'm suffocating under the weight of my responsibilities. It makes me anxious, and I worry that if I don't meet everyone's expectations, I'll be seen as a failure.
I find myself constantly replaying memories with my aunt in my mind, and it makes me feel so lonely and sad. I can't seem to move past it, and I just feel stuck.

When I had to skip the movie to support my friend, I felt a mix of anxiety and disappointment. I wanted to enjoy my time, but I thought I had to put her needs first, and that made me feel bad about myself.
